translation.literal_translation: Now, therefore, the desire to know Brahman.
translation.technical_translation: Therefore, following the requisite preparation, inquiry into Brahman is to be undertaken.
translation.interpretive_note: The compact opening supports two linked readings: (1) a strict nominal reading (brahma-jijnasa as desire/inquiry regarding Brahman), and (2) a procedural reading in which atha/atah signals qualified transition into Vedanta inquiry.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Marks formal commencement and transition into the Brahma inquiry section.
Signals inferential or consequential force: the inquiry follows from prior qualification and discernment.
Object of inquiry in the opening adhikarana; later specified through causal-lakshana in the next sutra.
Not mere curiosity; a disciplined epistemic undertaking directed toward Brahman.

uncertainty_notes: Atha/atah force can be distributed differently across literal vs technical renderings.
Final publication pass should verify sandhi resolution and punctuation against a critical print edition.
